Now that we've covered each of the principles of persuasion, let's take a moment to revisit one of the most important topics of this entire training, ethics. Recall that in the workshop section on ethics we saw how Bernie Madoff abused several principles of persuasion and how he and those around him suffered greatly for it. Which of the principles of persuasion did Madoff abuse and smuggle into his interactions with others? Madoff leveraged his authority in the finance industry, amplified by his expertise in perceived trustworthiness. He also made use of social proof by recruiting people into his schemes who were similar to his recruiters and to many others who had already joined Madoff's font. But these smuggler tactics led to Madoff's ruin and to substantial harm to those around him. As the commentator Ben Stein stated, personal relationships are the fertile soil from which all advancement, all success, all achievement in real life grows. With this in mind we must always strive to apply these powerful principles of persuasion in ways that are true, natural to the situation and wise for all concerned. To do otherwise invites misfortune for all concerned. I wish you a great and influential future.
